steps.title: Move from general to exact
steps.content: The first sentence says elephants ranked higher than crocodiles. The next sentence gives the exact rankings, so the transition should introduce a precise detail that supports the general comparison.

steps.title: Reject wrong transitions
steps.content: Choice A signals a consequence, but no conclusion is being drawn. Choice C signals contrast, though the rankings confirm the comparison. Choice D signals time, which is irrelevant here.

steps.title: Introduce the rankings
steps.content: Choice B signals specificity: elephants ranked third and crocodiles fifteenth, which precisely illustrates the earlier statement that elephants ranked higher. The answer is B.
